title: Congratulating the Los Angeles Dodgers for winning the 2024 Major League Baseball World Series.
plain_summary: This bill is a congratulatory message from Congress to the Los Angeles Dodgers baseball team. The Dodgers won the 2024 World Series by beating the New York Yankees. The bill recognizes the team's great performance and mentions special players like Freddie Freeman and Shohei Ohtani. It also honors the memory of Fernando Valenzuela, a former Dodgers player who died right before the World Series started.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.

sections.title: Resolution Congratulating the Los Angeles Dodgers for winning the 2024 Major League Baseball World Series
sections.text: On October thirtieth, two thousand twenty-four, the Los Angeles Dodgers beat the New York Yankees seven to six to win the two thousand twenty-four Major League Baseball World Series in five games. This victory was the eighth World Series championship for the Dodgers team and their seventh title since they moved from Brooklyn to Los Angeles in nineteen fifty-eight. During the two thousand twenty-four playoffs, the Dodgers beat the San Diego Padres, the New York Mets, and the New York Yankees on their way to winning the World Series. After falling behind by five runs in Game five of the World Series, the Dodgers showed great determination by scoring five runs of their own in an exciting fifth-inning comeback that led to their series-winning victory. The Dodgers have won their division eleven out of the last twelve seasons, won the National League pennant (championship of their league) four out of the last eight seasons, and won the World Series two out of the last five seasons. The Dodgers finished the two thousand twenty-four regular season with an impressive record of ninety-eight wins and sixty-four losses to finish first in the National League West Division and with the best regular season record in all of baseball. The Dodgers defense tied a Major League Baseball playoff record with thirty-three straight innings without allowing the other team to score. The New York Yankees, who played against the Dodgers in the two thousand twenty-four World Series, showed good sportsmanship and respect for the game. Every member of the two thousand twenty-four Dodgers roster played an important part in winning the World Series during this historic season. The resolution then lists all thirty-six players by name. While dealing with injury and challenges, Dodgers first baseman Freddie Freeman was named Most Valuable Player for the two thousand twenty-four World Series and batted three hundred with four home runs and twelve runs batted in, including a walk-off grand slam in Game one of the World Series on October twenty-fifth, two thousand twenty-four. Shohei Ohtani had one of the best regular seasons in baseball history, hitting three hundred ten with fifty-four home runs, one hundred thirty-four runs scored, and one hundred thirty runs batted in, all while stealing fifty-nine bases. This made him the first player in Major League Baseball history to have at least fifty home runs and fifty stolen bases in the same season. Mookie Betts recovered from a midseason injury before returning to play a key role in the Dodgers' two thousand twenty-four postseason, earning his third World Series ring. Teoscar Hernandez had an impressive regular season and postseason, was selected to the two thousand twenty-four Major League Baseball All-Star Game, and won the Major League Baseball Home Run Derby, becoming the first Dodger ever to win that contest. Dodger fans were deeply saddened by the death of Fernando Valenzuela just days before the start of the two thousand twenty-four World Series. Valenzuela created a special connection between the team and the Latino community of Los Angeles and will always be remembered by all Dodgers fans. Dodgers manager Dave Roberts won his second World Series Championship with the Dodgers. Dodgers fans never stopped supporting the team throughout the two thousand twenty-four season, playing an important role in motivating their team to victory. Therefore, the House of Representatives congratulates the Los Angeles Dodgers for winning the two thousand twenty-four Major League Baseball World Series, recognizes the achievements and contributions of the entire Los Angeles Dodgers organization including the players, coaches, management, and support staff in bringing the Commissioner's Trophy (the World Series championship trophy) to Los Angeles, and directs the Clerk of the House of Representatives to send an official copy of this resolution to the chairman and controlling owner of the Los Angeles Dodgers Mark Walter, the president of the Los Angeles Dodgers Stan Kasten, and the manager of the two thousand twenty-four Los Angeles Dodgers Dave Roberts.
